title: How to Use the Hot Tub
content:
## Hot Tub Instructions

1. **Remove the cover** - Fold it back onto the cover lifter
2. **Check temperature** - Should be 38-40°C
3. **Turn on jets** - Press the green button on the control panel
4. **Enjoy!** - Maximum 30 minutes recommended
5. **When finished** - Turn off jets, replace the cover

### Important
- Shower before entering
- No glass near the hot tub
- Children must be supervised
- Cover must always be closed when not in use

title: Heating Controls
content:
## Cabin Heating

The cabin uses underfloor heating controlled by thermostats.

### Main Thermostat (Living Room)
- Located on the wall near the entrance
- Set to **21°C** by default
- Adjust using + and - buttons
- Changes take 30-60 minutes to take effect

### Bedroom Thermostat
- Located on the wall by the door
- Set to **19°C** by default
- We recommend keeping at 18-20°C for sleeping

### Fireplace
- Firewood is in the basket by the fireplace
- Matches and fire starters are on the mantle
- Open the flue before lighting
- Never leave unattended

title: Kitchen Guide
content:
## Kitchen Guide

### Appliances
- **Coffee machine**: Nespresso - pods in the drawer below
- **Oven**: Fan-assisted, preheat for 10 minutes
- **Dishwasher**: Tablets under the sink, run before you leave
- **Induction hob**: Use only compatible pans (magnetic base)

### Basics Provided
- Coffee and tea
- Salt, pepper, and basic spices
- Olive oil and butter
- Milk (check fridge)

### Waste & Recycling
- **Blue bin**: Plastics and metal
- **Green bin**: Paper and cardboard
- **Black bin**: General waste
- **Compost**: Food scraps in the brown bin

title: WiFi
content: Network: NordurljosGuest. Password: <redacted>. Signal is strongest in the living room. If the connection drops, unplug the white router by the TV for 10 seconds and plug it back in.

title: BBQ Grill
content: Gas grill on the front deck. Turn the red knob to open gas, press the ignition button. Clean the grate with the wire brush after use. Spare propane tank in the utility room.

title: Free Parking
content: Park in the gravel lot next to the cabin. Space for 2 cars. In winter, please keep the driveway edge clear for the snow plow.

title: Kitchen
content: Fully equipped with oven, stovetop, microwave, coffee maker, and dishwasher. Pots, pans, and utensils in the lower cabinets. Please run the dishwasher before checkout.

title: Fireplace
content: Wood-burning fireplace in the living room. Firewood stacked outside the front door. Open the flue damper (metal lever above the opening) before lighting. Close the glass doors once the fire is going. Never leave unattended.

title: Washing Machine
content: In the utility room behind the kitchen. Use the eco cycle (button 3) for normal loads. Detergent pods are in the basket on top. Max 6 kg.

title: Hot Tub
content: The hot tub is on the back deck at 39°C. Please shower before entering. Close the insulated cover when not in use. The red valve on the left controls water flow — turn it halfway only.

title: sauna
content:
To operate the sauna make first sure the emergency flip inside the sauna is set to ON. 
After that press the ON button on lower left corner on the controller, located on the shower wall outside of the sauna. 
There you can set your desired temps as well, the sauna is pre-set to turn off after 1 hour, but the sauna oven stays warm longer
Note ! 
It takes about 30 min for the sauna to fully heat up
There is crane for water on the same wall as the controller for the sauna or you can use the shower head as well 
Bucket, ladle and a sauna fan is inside the sauna, after oven heats up you pour water carefully on the stones in the sauna oven. 
If the sauna fan is out of battery it can be charged with a standar c cable sama as for phones, it takes short time to charge. 
NO FOOD OR DRINK INISDE THE SAUNA BESIDES A STILL WATER 
Use responible and according to good practices, use of the spa facilites is optional and on your own risk

title: Door lock on main entrance
content: There is a 3 bolt lock on the door, to be able to lock the door behind you, you will need to lift the doorhandle at the same time you turn the key ! Please keep the key in the key box while away so it will not get lost :)

title: Stove Top
content: There is a child lock feature on the stove top, if it activates a red light in shape of a key appears, press the icon and hold for 3 sec to unlock

title: Hot tub (Polarspa)
content:
There is a touch screen control on the Jacuzzi, to activate the controls then : 
1. Slide finger across the screen to activate 
2. Drag the 2 icons that appear together to unlock the controls 
3. You will only need 2 options here, the arrow keys in centre control the temp, max temp to set is 40 Celsius, and the three 000 icons on top, the one to the left turn on/off the jets (X00)
4. there is a Bluetooth speaker inbuilt in the jacuzzi, it connects via Phone but if you use it please be respectful to other guest and use on low volume, thank you 
5.	After using the Jacuzzi please secure the lid back on top with the straps provided, as wind destroy it quick and in Iceland weather can change every minute, note if neglecting this rule leading to damage of the top lid can result in damage fee up to 1000 USD ! This will also make sure the temperature stays warm and cosy for your next dive :)  

Please shower before entering the hot tub. Do not use soap, shampoo, lotions, or other products in the tub. This keeps the water clean for everyone and if not fallowed may result in charges of continamation fee 
there is no food allowed in the Jacuzzi !
Use of the spa facility is optional and always on your risk, beware that the surroundings of the Jacuzzi can get slippery

title: Heatpump
content: To use the Heat Pump for heating or cooling you will need to operate it with the remote hanging on the wall next to the bathroom door, while changing setting you need to point the remote directly at the heat pump

title: Dishwasher
content:
Note there is 3 drawers in the dishwasher! If you pull out the one on top then you can load the cutlery in there ! pods are provided in a jar under the kitchen sink, the jar is marked "Dish" (the other jar marked "laundry" has pods for the laundry machine)
1. load the pod into the Gray chamber inside the machine door and close the chamber
2. turn on the machine on top of the machine door and select washing program 
3. Close the door and the machine will soon turn on the washing program by it self

title: Nespresso Coffey maker
content:
Pods for the Nespresso Coffey maker are in big jar on the shelf above the Coffey maker, 
1. Make sure there is enough water in the water storage in back of the machine, there is brown water can on the shelf to easy fill up the water chamber if needed
2. Open the Nespresso by sliding the shift on front to the side 
3. Load the coffey pod and close the machine (flat part with alumnium points upwards on the pod) 
4. Put your cup under and press the on button 
5. Enjoy your coffey :)

title: Washing machine / dryer
content:
There are pods for the washing machine under the kitchen sink in a jar marked "laundry"  (There is another jar at the same place marked "dish" that has pods for the dishwasher) 
1. load the machine with clothes, and put 1 pod into the tumbler 
2. select program 
3. turn on
No smoking inside the cabin
Please remove shoes at the entrance
Quiet hours: 23:00 - 07:00
Hot tub cover must be closed with provided strap after use! heavy winds can appear on short notice and blow the lid away, can result in damage charge!
Separate waste into recycling bins provided
No pets allowed
Maximum 2 guests per cabin (+infant)

Privacy of other Guests, Please do not climb on the hills next to your house, these hills are made to separate the houses and give privacy to both you and other guests.  Respect the privacy of guests staying in other cabins and quiet hours !
Drone use, Drone use is forbidden at the property, but can be allowed under special requirements, always reach out to us for permission to use a drone ahead!
Maximum 4 guest per cabin (+infant)
No food or drinks beside still water inside the sauna chambers !
No food or added continements in the Jacuzzi such as (not limited to) oils, soaps, bath bombs etc
Its forbidden to climb on the grass hills next to Glass cabins, these hills are made for privacy of the guest, respect other guests privacy at all times!
No smoking inside the cabin
Please remove shoes at the entrance
Quiet hours: 23:00 - 07:00
Hot tub cover must be closed after use
Separate waste into recycling bins provided
Maximum 6 guests per cabin

title: LAVA Centre
content:
Award-winning interactive exhibition about Iceland's volcanic activity. Walk through earthquake simulators and learn about the 2010 Eyjafjallajökull eruption that happened right next to your cabin!

A must-visit! The rooftop view is incredible on a clear day.

title: Gljúfrabúi (The Hidden Waterfall)
content:
A secret waterfall hidden inside a narrow canyon, just 500m from Seljalandsfoss. Wade through a shallow stream into the cave to see the falls pour through the opening above. Magical!

My secret favorite! Most tourists miss this one. Bring waterproof everything.

title: Gluggafoss (Window Waterfall)
content:
A fascinating waterfall where water has carved tunnels through the rock, creating natural 'windows'. Much less crowded than Seljalandsfoss. A peaceful 20 min walk from parking.

Beautiful and peaceful. You'll likely have it all to yourself!

title: Seljalandsfoss Waterfall
content:
One of Iceland's most iconic waterfalls - you can walk behind the 60m cascade! Best visited in golden hour. Bring a waterproof jacket. Just 15 min from the cabin.

Walk behind the falls for an unforgettable experience! Get there early to avoid crowds.

title: Hvolsvöllur Swimming Pool
content:
Geothermally heated outdoor pool with hot tubs, water slide, and mountain views. The most Icelandic experience you can have for 1000 ISK. Open year-round.

Go in the evening for a soak under the stars. Absolute bliss.

title: Saga Centre
content:
Museum dedicated to the Njáls Saga, one of Iceland's greatest literary works. Interactive exhibits about Viking-age life in this very valley.

Fascinating if you're into history. The saga took place right in this area.

title: Drangurinn Rock
content:
A mysterious giant boulder sitting below the Eyjafjöll mountains. Legend says a farmer once lived on top of it. Strange, beautiful, and totally free to visit.

Just 5 min drive! Look for the tiny house ruins on top. So quirky.

title: Dyrhólaey Arch
content: Dramatic sea arch and lighthouse with panoramic views. Great for puffin watching in summer (May-August).

title: Krónan Supermarket Vik
content: The main supermarket in Vik. Good selection of groceries, fresh produce, and Icelandic specialties.

title: N1 Gas Station Vik
content: 24-hour gas station with a small shop. Last gas station before long stretch of Ring Road heading east.

title: Kötlujökull Ice Cave Trail
content:
A lesser-known trail leading to small ice formations near Kötlujökull. Not as crowded as the commercial tours.

Only attempt this with proper hiking boots and check weather conditions first.

title: Seljavallalaug Hot Spring
content:
A hidden 25m swimming pool built into the mountainside in 1923. One of Iceland's best-kept secrets. 20-minute hike in.

My favorite hidden gem! The hike is easy and the pool is magical. Go early morning for fewer crowds.

title: Katla Geopark Visitor Centre (Brydebúð)
content: Visitor centre in Vík with geology, Katla volcano and local history.

title: Reynisfjara Black Sand Beach
content:
One of the most beautiful non-tropical beaches in the world. Watch out for sneaker waves!

Never turn your back to the ocean here. The sneaker waves are real and dangerous.

title: Seljalandsfoss Waterfall
content:
A stunning 60-meter waterfall where you can walk behind the cascade. 10 min drive west of Sky Retreat Iceland Cabins

We recommend going during the midnight sun ! Don´t forget about the neighbour Glúfrabúi waterfall!

title: Hjörleifshöfði Cave
content:
Dramatic cave at the base of a headland and connected to Viking settlement history.

Amazing place! The walk is across soft black sand so give yourself extra time.

title: Seljalandsfoss
content: A 60-metre waterfall with a path that lets you walk behind the cascade.

title: Gljúfrabúi
content: Hidden waterfall tucked inside a mossy canyon, 200 m north of Seljalandsfoss.

title: Skógafoss
content: Iconic 60-metre wide cascade with a staircase leading up to the top viewpoint.

title: Kvernufoss
content: Quiet waterfall in a small canyon behind Skógar museum, a short walk away.

title: Reynisfjara Black Sand Beach
content: Black sand beach with basalt columns and powerful sneaker waves — caution.

title: Dyrhólaey
content: Sea arch promontory with lighthouse, puffins in summer and sweeping views.

title: Reynisdrangar Sea Stacks
content: Three basalt sea stacks rising from the Atlantic off the Reynisfjara coast.

title: Vík í Mýrdal Beach
content: Black sand beach in Vík village with views of Reynisdrangar sea stacks.

title: Sólheimajökull Glacier
content: Accessible outlet glacier of Mýrdalsjökull — guided ice hikes start here.

title: Mýrdalsjökull Viewpoint
content: Roadside view of the ice cap that covers the Katla volcano.

title: Eyjafjallajökull Visitor Centre
content: Farm visitor centre on Route 1 with films about the 2010 eruption.

title: Seljavallalaug
content: Old mountain pool from 1923 in a valley, 20-minute walk from the road.

title: Nauthúsagil
content: Narrow mossy ravine with a hidden waterfall, accessed by wading and ropes.

title: Stakkholtsgjá Canyon
content: Dramatic 100 m deep canyon in Þórsmörk with a waterfall at the end.

title: Gullfoss
content: Two-tiered waterfall on the Hvítá river, a Golden Circle highlight.

title: Geysir & Strokkur
content: Active geothermal area where Strokkur erupts every 5–10 minutes.

title: Þingvellir National Park
content: UNESCO site at the rift between tectonic plates, founding place of Alþingi.

title: Öxarárfoss
content: Waterfall in Þingvellir tumbling into the Almannagjá rift.

title: Kerið Crater
content: Volcanic crater lake with red slopes, short walk around the rim.

title: Brúarfoss
content: Bright turquoise small waterfall reached via a marked trail by Hlauptunga.

title: Faxi (Vatnsleysufoss)
content: Wide gentle waterfall on the Tungufljót, with a salmon ladder beside it.

title: Hjálparfoss
content: Double waterfall framed by basalt columns at the edge of Þjórsárdalur.

title: Háifoss
content: 122 m tall waterfall in Þjórsárdalur — one of Iceland's highest.

title: Granni
content: Slender waterfall right beside Háifoss, sharing the same canyon viewpoint.

title: Gjáin
content: Lush ravine with small waterfalls and pools, near the Stöng farm site.

title: Þjófafoss
content: Wide waterfall on the Þjórsá river with Búrfell mountain as backdrop.

title: Urriðafoss
content: Iceland's most voluminous waterfall by flow, easy stop just off Route 1.

title: Hrunalaug
content: Tiny natural hot spring near Flúðir — please be respectful and tidy.

title: Reykjadalur Hot River
content: Geothermal valley above Hveragerði with a warm bathing river, ~1 hr hike.

title: Almannagjá Gorge
content: 7 km long fissure on the Mid-Atlantic rift, walked from Þingvellir P1.

title: Silfra Fissure
content: Crystal-clear fissure between tectonic plates, a top snorkelling and dive site.

title: Þingvallavatn
content: Iceland's largest natural lake, framed by the Þingvellir rift valley.

title: Hveragerði Geothermal Park
content: Town-centre geothermal park where you can boil eggs in a hot spring.

title: Hellisheiði Lava Field
content: Vast moss-covered lava field crossed by Route 1 between Reykjavík and Selfoss.

title: Eyrarbakki Beach
content: Long Atlantic shoreline with seabirds and views to Vestmannaeyjar.

title: Stokkseyri Coast
content: Rocky shoreline village with seal colonies and birdlife along the south coast.

title: Þórsmörk Valley
content: Mountain valley between three glaciers — F-road access, 4x4 only.

title: Valahnúkur
content: Easy 458 m peak in Þórsmörk with panoramic glacier views from the top.

title: Tindfjöll
content: Tinted ridge above Þórsmörk with sweeping valley views from the trail.

title: Hekla Viewpoint
content: Roadside viewpoint of the active Hekla volcano, the 'Gateway to Hell'.

title: Tröllkonuhlaup
content: Narrow river canyon on Þjórsá with footbridge and lava cliffs.

title: Gígjökull
content: Outlet glacier of Eyjafjallajökull whose lagoon vanished in the 2010 eruption.

title: Þjórsárver Edge Viewpoint
content: Edge of Þjórsá basin near Búrfell power plant, with forested walking paths.

title: Markarfljót Canyon Viewpoint
content: Deep glacial river canyon viewed from the Þórsmörk road.

title: Drumbabót Petrified Forest
content: Stumps of a 9th-century birch forest in the Þverá riverbed near Hvolsvöllur.

title: Þrengsli Lava Road
content: Scenic lava-field route between Þorlákshöfn and Route 1.

title: Vík Church Hill
content: Hill above Vík with the church and panoramic views of Reynisdrangar.

title: Mýrdalssandur
content: Vast black outwash plain east of Vík, formed by Katla's jökulhlaups.

title: Tungufljót Canyon
content: Small river gorge near Reykholt with viewing platform.

title: Sundhöll Selfoss
content: Town pool with indoor/outdoor lanes, slides, hot tubs and steam — open year-round.

title: Sundlaugin Laugaskarði
content: Hveragerði's outdoor pool with hot tubs, a sauna and a steam room.

title: Laugarvatn Fontana
content: Geothermal baths and natural steam rooms on the shore of Lake Laugarvatn.

title: Secret Lagoon (Gamla Laugin)
content: Iceland's oldest swimming pool, built 1891 — natural hot springs in Flúðir.

title: Sundlaugin Flúðum
content: Local outdoor pool in Flúðir with hot tubs, by the school sports centre.

title: Sundlaugin Laugarvatni
content: 25 m village pool with three hot tubs, cold pot and steam bath.

title: Sundlaugin Hellu
content: Outdoor town pool with hot tubs and slides in central Hella.

title: Sundlaugin Hvolsvelli
content: Heated outdoor pool with slide and hot tubs in Hvolsvöllur.

title: Sundlaugin í Vík
content: Compact village pool with hot tubs and small slide in Vík í Mýrdal.

title: Sundlaug Þorlákshafnar
content: Þorlákshöfn town pool with hot tubs, slide and steam bath.

title: Sundlaug Stokkseyrar
content: Small village pool with hot tubs in Stokkseyri.

title: Reykholtslaug
content: Country pool in Reykholt, Bláskógabyggð — check status, renovation 2025–26.

title: Seljavallalaug Natural Pool
content: 1923 stone pool in a valley below Eyjafjallajökull — free, basic, lukewarm.

title: Almannagjá Trail
content: Easy paved walk along the Mid-Atlantic rift to Öxarárfoss waterfall.

title: Reykjadalur Hot Spring Hike
content: Moderate 7 km return hike from Hveragerði to a warm bathing river.

title: Skógafoss Waterfall Trail
content: Stair climb to Skógafoss viewpoint then 20+ waterfalls along the Skógá river.

title: Fimmvörðuháls Pass
content: Demanding 25 km hike from Skógar to Þórsmörk over a glacier pass.

title: Laugavegur Trail Start (Þórsmörk end)
content: Southern end of the famed 55 km Landmannalaugar–Þórsmörk multi-day trek.

title: Valahnúkur Loop (Þórsmörk)
content: Short 458 m peak ascent in Þórsmörk with 360° glacier panorama.

title: Tindfjöll Ridge
content: Ridge walk above Þórsmörk with sweeping views to Eyjafjallajökull.

title: Kattarhryggir Ridge
content: Narrow exposed ridge trail in Þórsmörk — for sure-footed hikers.

title: Brúarfoss Trail
content: 3 km marked path from new car park to bright turquoise Brúarfoss.

title: Stakkholtsgjá Canyon Walk
content: Easy 2 km walk into a deep canyon with a waterfall at the end.

title: Háifoss & Granni Viewpoint Walk
content: Short 1 km stroll from the parking to the canyon rim above Háifoss.

title: Hekla Summit Route
content: Demanding ascent of the active Hekla volcano — only with current safety info.

title: Drumbabót Walk
content: Easy summer riverbed walk to the 9th-century petrified birch stumps.

title: LAVA Centre
content: Award-winning interactive volcano and earthquake exhibition in Hvolsvöllur.

title: Skógar Museum (Skógasafn)
content: Folk, transport and turf-house museum next to Skógafoss.

title: Þjóðveldisbærinn Stöng
content: Reconstructed Viking-era farm in Þjórsárdalur, buried by Hekla in 1104.

title: Saga Centre (Sagnaheimur Njálu)
content: Exhibition on Njáls saga and Viking-age life in Hvolsvöllur.

title: Bobby Fischer Center
content: Small museum on chess world champion Bobby Fischer, buried near Selfoss.

title: Eyrarbakki Maritime Museum
content: Coastal village museum on fishing heritage, with a 12-oared rowing boat.

title: Húsið á Eyrarbakka
content: 1765 wooden merchant house and folk museum, Iceland's oldest dwelling.

title: Friðheimar Tomato Greenhouse
content: Geothermal greenhouse with tomato lunch and Icelandic horse shows.

title: Skálholt Cathedral
content: Historic bishops' seat from 1056, current cathedral built in 1963.

title: Strandarkirkja
content: Coastal church at Selvogur known for miraculous donations from sailors.

title: Stokkseyri Ghost Centre
content: Folklore museum of Icelandic ghosts and hauntings — currently closed, check.

title: Icelandic Wonders
content: Stokkseyri exhibition on Icelandic elves, trolls and northern lights.

title: Keldur Turf Farm
content: One of Iceland's oldest turf farms, mentioned in Njáls saga, south of Hella.

title: Oddi Church
content: Historic church site at Oddi, learning centre of Sæmundur fróði.

title: Þingvellir Visitor Centre (Hakið)
content: Park visitor centre at Hakið viewpoint with exhibition and rift overlook.

title: Geysir Center
content: Visitor and shopping centre opposite the Geysir geothermal area.

title: Gullfoss Visitor Centre
content: Café and shop on the rim above Gullfoss waterfall.

title: Slakki Petting Zoo
content: Family petting zoo in Laugarás with farm animals and minigolf.

title: Ljósafossstöð Power Exhibition
content: Free interactive exhibition on geothermal and hydro power inside a station.

title: Brytinn Art Gallery
content: Small contemporary art gallery and design shop in Selfoss centre.

title: Katla Geopark Visitor Centre (Brydebúð)
content: Visitor centre in Vík with geology, Katla volcano and local history.

title: Sögusafnið – Þorvaldseyri Visitor Centre
content: Family farm visitor centre with film about the 2010 Eyjafjallajökull eruption.

title: N1 Þjónustustöð Selfoss
content: Full-service N1 station with restaurant, shop, car wash and 24/7 self-service.

title: N1 Þjónustustöð Hveragerði
content: 24/7 N1 service station on Breiðumörk with shop and car wash.

title: N1 Þjónustustöð Hvolsvöllur
content: 24/7 N1 station with restaurant, shop and electric chargers in Hvolsvöllur.

title: N1 Sjálfsafgreiðsla Vík
content: Self-service N1 pumps in Vík village, near the church hill.

title: N1 Sjálfsafgreiðsla Flúðir
content: Self-service N1 station in Flúðir village by Grund.

title: N1 Sjálfsafgreiðsla Laugarvatn
content: Self-service N1 pumps at Dalbraut in Laugarvatn village.

title: N1 Sjálfsafgreiðsla Geysir
content: Pump cluster at the Geysir geothermal area on Route 35.

title: Olís Þjónustustöð Selfoss
content: Olís service station on Austurvegur with food and convenience shop.

title: Olís Þjónustustöð Hella
content: Full-service Olís station on Suðurlandsvegur in Hella with shop.

title: ÓB Selfoss
content: Self-service discount pumps behind the Olís Selfoss station.

title: ÓB Eyrarbakki
content: Self-service discount pumps in Eyrarbakki coastal village.

title: ÓB Þorlákshöfn
content: Self-service discount pumps in Þorlákshöfn near the harbour.

title: ÓB Minni-Borg
content: Self-service pumps at Minni-Borg, between Selfoss and Reykholt.

title: ÓB Landvegamót
content: Self-service pumps at the Landvegamót crossroads east of Hella.

title: ÓB Vík
content: Self-service discount pumps on Smiðjuvegur in Vík.

title: Orkan Selfoss
content: 24/7 self-service Orkan station on the Ring Road bypass east of Selfoss.

title: Orkan Austurmörk Hveragerði
content: 24/7 Orkan self-service with fast EV charger on Austurmörk in Hveragerði.

title: Orkan Sunnumörk Hveragerði
content: 24/7 Orkan X self-service station at Sunnumörk in Hveragerði.

title: Orkan Þorlákshöfn
content: 24/7 self-service Orkan station at Óseyrarbraut in Þorlákshöfn.

title: Orkan Stokkseyri
content: 24/7 Orkan self-service pumps on Hásteinsvegur in Stokkseyri.

title: Orkan Hella
content: 24/7 Orkan self-service station at Faxaflatir industrial area, Hella.

title: Orkan Hvolsvöllur
content: 24/7 Orkan self-service pumps on Austurvegur in Hvolsvöllur.

title: Orkan Vík
content: 24/7 Orkan self-service station on Smiðjuvegur in Vík.

title: Bónus Selfoss
content: Discount supermarket on Larsenstræti — Iceland's cheapest grocery chain.

title: Bónus Hveragerði
content: Discount supermarket at Sunnumörk shopping cluster in Hveragerði.

title: Krónan Selfoss
content: Large discount supermarket on Austurvegur — open daily 8–21.

title: Krónan Vík
content: Main supermarket in Vík, on Austurvegur near the village centre.

title: Nettó Selfoss (Austurvegur)
content: Discount supermarket on Austurvegur, west end of Selfoss town.

title: Nettó Selfoss (Eyravegur)
content: Newer 1000 m² Nettó on Eyravegur, opened December 2024.

title: Hagkaup Selfoss
content: Full-range supermarket sharing the Larsenstræti complex with Bónus.

title: Krambúðin Selfoss
content: Convenience store on Tryggvagata, often open long hours.

title: Krambúðin Flúðir
content: Village convenience store in Flúðir with basic groceries and OTC medicines.

title: Krambúðin Laugarvatn
content: Village shop in Laugarvatn carrying basic groceries and OTC medicines.

title: Kjörbúðin Hella
content: Main supermarket in Hella, operated by Samkaup.

title: Strax Þorlákshöfn
content: Convenience grocery store in Þorlákshöfn town centre.

title: Kjörbúðin Hvolsvelli
content: Local supermarket in Hvolsvöllur, Samkaup chain.

title: Heilbrigðisstofnun Suðurlands – Selfoss
content: Regional hospital and 24-hour emergency department for South Iceland.

title: HSU Heilsugæslan Hveragerði
content: Hveragerði primary care clinic — book via heilsuvera.is.

title: HSU Heilsugæslan Þorlákshöfn
content: Primary care clinic in Þorlákshöfn — book via heilsuvera.is.

title: HSU Heilsugæslan Laugarási
content: Rural clinic serving the upper Árnessýsla region (Reykholt, Flúðir, Laugarvatn).

title: HSU Heilsugæslan Hella
content: Primary care clinic in Hella for Rangárþing ytra.

title: HSU Heilsugæslan Hvolsvelli
content: Primary care clinic in Hvolsvöllur — book via heilsuvera.is.

title: HSU Heilsugæslan Vík
content: Primary care clinic in Vík with after-hours on-call number.

title: Lyfja Selfoss
content: Pharmacy on Austurvegur with hearing services and vaccinations.

title: Lyf og heilsa Selfossi
content: Pharmacy inside the Krónan building on Austurvegur.

title: Apótek Suðurlands
content: Independent pharmacy on Austurvegur, Selfoss.

title: Lyf og heilsa Hveragerði
content: Pharmacy on Breiðumörk in Hveragerði town centre.

title: Lyf og heilsa Þorlákshöfn
content: Pharmacy on Selvogsbraut, co-located with the health clinic.

title: Lyf og heilsa Hella
content: Pharmacy on Suðurlandsvegur in Hella service centre.

title: Lyf og heilsa Hvolsvöllur
content: Pharmacy on Austurvegur in Hvolsvöllur — limited hours.

title: Icewear Apótek Vík
content: Limited-range OTC pharmacy counter at Icewear store in Vík.

title: Landsbankinn Selfoss
content: Branch and ATM on Austurvegur, the main bank office in Selfoss.

title: Íslandsbanki Selfoss
content: Branch and ATM in central Selfoss on Austurvegur.

title: Landsbankinn Hella
content: Branch and ATM at the Hella service centre.

title: Landsbankinn Hvolsvöllur
content: Branch and ATM in Hvolsvöllur — please confirm hours before visiting.

title: Pósturinn Selfoss
content: Iceland Post service point on Tryggvagata in Selfoss.

title: Pósturinn Hveragerði
content: Iceland Post service point in the Sunnumörk shopping centre.

title: Pósturinn Hella
content: Iceland Post service point in the Hella service centre.

title: Pósturinn Hvolsvöllur
content: Iceland Post service point on Austurvegur in Hvolsvöllur.

title: Pósturinn Vík
content: Iceland Post service point in Vík village centre.

title: Pósturinn Þorlákshöfn
content: Iceland Post service point on Hafnarberg in Þorlákshöfn.

title: HSU Heilsugæslan Vestmannaeyjabraut Selfoss (ATM)
content: Arion banki ATM in central Selfoss on Austurvegur.

title: Hygge Restaurant & Bar
content:
Fusion of Icelandic and international cuisine. Amazing terrace views of Eyjafjallajökull and Vestmannaeyjar islands. Try the lamb shoulder.

Best restaurant in the area! Book ahead in summer.

title: Midgard Restaurant & Bar
content:
Hearty meals at the adventure base camp. Great burgers, fish and chips, and local beers on tap. Popular with hikers returning from Þórsmörk.

Great casual spot. Their lamb burger is amazing.

title: Eldstó Art Café
content:
Charming café with homemade cakes, fish stew, and art exhibitions. Cozy atmosphere in a beautiful old building. Perfect for a rainy day.

My go-to for coffee and cake. The fish stew is hearty.

title: SVO Restaurant & Bar
content:
Elegant restaurant at Hotel Hvolsvöllur. Fresh fish of the day, tender lamb, and homemade burgers. Warm and inviting atmosphere.

Excellent for a nice dinner out. The fish of the day never disappoints.

title: Björkin
content:
Local fast food and convenience shop. Great pulled pork burgers and quick bites. Open late, perfect for a quick meal.

Grab something quick. The pulled pork burger is surprisingly good!

title: Faxi Bakery
content:
Charming coffee shop and bakery. Excellent coffee, homemade cakes, and light bites.

Perfect morning stop for coffee. Their cinnamon rolls are legendary.

title: Skálakot Restaurant
content:
Modern bistro with a creative menu blending Icelandic ingredients with international flavors. Great cocktail menu.

Great for a special dinner. Reserve ahead in summer, it gets busy.

title: Black Crust Pizzeria
content:
Craft brewery and restaurant with wood-fired pizzas and local beers on tap. Relaxed atmosphere.

Best pizza in south Iceland and they brew their own beer. Try the Vikingur lager!

title: Hotel Anna restaurant
content:
A cozy, family-run restaurant offering traditional Icelandic cuisine made with local ingredients. Enjoy dishes like pan-fried arctic char and lamb fillet in a warm countryside setting with mountain views. Á La Carte Restaurat that offers take away and walk in breakfast only 2km away from your location!

Offer take away to enjoy right in your cabin ! 
In winter call ahead if you plan to go to breakfast

title: Eyjafjallajökull Snowmobile Adventure
content: Ride a snowmobile across the glacier that stopped Europe's air traffic in 2010! Stunning views of the highlands and south coast from the ice cap.

title: Seljalandsfoss & Gljúfrabúi Waterfall Walk
content: Walk behind the famous Seljalandsfoss waterfall, then discover the hidden Gljúfrabúi waterfall tucked inside a narrow canyon. Just 15 min from the cabin!

title: Þórsmörk Valley Hiking
content: Explore Iceland's most beautiful valley, nestled between three glaciers. Dramatic canyon views, lush greenery, and unforgettable landscapes. Requires 4x4 river crossing.

title: Glacier Hiking on Sólheimajökull
content: Explore the stunning ice formations of Sólheimajökull glacier with certified glacier guides. Crampons, ice axes, and all equipment provided. 45 min drive east.

title: Northern Lights Hunt
content: Chase the aurora borealis with expert guides who know the darkest spots in the Eyjafjöll area. Hot chocolate and Icelandic kleinur included. Sept-March only.

title: Icelandic Horse Riding in Eyjafjöll
content: Experience the gentle Icelandic horse and its unique tölt gait on a scenic ride with Eyjafjallajökull glacier as your backdrop. Suitable for all levels.

title: Northern Lights Adventure
content: Chase the aurora borealis with expert guides who know the best spots away from light pollution. Includes hot chocolate and warm blankets.

title: Golden Circle Classic
content: Visit Iceland's most iconic natural wonders: Þingvellir National Park, Geysir geothermal area, and the magnificent Gullfoss waterfall.

title: Glacier Hiking on Sólheimajökull
content: Explore the stunning ice formations of Sólheimajökull glacier with certified glacier guides. All equipment provided.

title: Whale Watching from Vik
content: Spot humpback whales, dolphins, and seabirds in the rich waters off the south coast. Seasonal availability.

title: Ice Cave Exploration
content: Venture into the magical blue ice caves of Mýrdalsjökull. A truly once-in-a-lifetime experience. Winter only (Nov-Mar).

title: Icelandic Horse Riding
content: Experience the unique Icelandic horse and its famous tölt gait on a scenic ride through volcanic landscapes near Vik.

title: Snorkeling in Silfra
content: Snorkel between the North American and Eurasian tectonic plates in crystal-clear glacial water at Silfra fissure in Þingvellir.

title: Checkout Reminder
content:
Checkout time is 11:00. Before leaving, please:
- Run the dishwasher
- Place towels in the laundry basket
- Close hot tub cover
- Lock the front door

Thank you for staying with us!

title: Northern Lights Forecast: High Activity Tonight!
content: The aurora forecast shows KP index of 5+ tonight. Best viewing time is between 22:00-02:00. The hot tub is a great spot to watch from! Remember to turn off all outdoor lights for the best experience.

title: Power outage in the area
content: We're currently experiencing a power outage. The electricity company is working on it and we expect it to be resolved shortly. Hot water, heating, and lighting may be affected. Thank you for your patience.

title: Northern Lights Forecast: High Activity Tonight!
content: The aurora forecast shows KP index of 5+ tonight. Best viewing time is between 22:00-02:00. The hot tub is a great spot to watch from! Remember to turn off all outdoor lights for the best experience.

title: Welcome to Nordurljós Cabin!
content: We're so happy to have you here! Make yourself at home. Check out the house guides for hot tub instructions and heating controls. Don't hesitate to reach out if you need anything!

title: Wi-Fi disruption
content: Our Wi-Fi is currently down. The internet provider is working to restore it. We expect it back within the hour.

title: Welcome to Skyretreat Iceland!
content: We're so happy to have you here! Make yourself at home. Check out the house guides for hot tub instructions and heating controls. Don't hesitate to reach out if you need anything.

title: Sky Retreat Iceland
content:
Welcome to Guestpad, your inhouse guide to your stay, if you scroll down this page a list of amenities appear, click on amenity and detailed guide on how to operate it appears ! Enjoy your stay with Sky Retreat Iceland

Pharmacy is located in Hvolsvöllur and Vík, opening hours may depend on seasons find more info online, doctor/health clinic is in Hvolsvöllur and Vík as well.

No smoking inside the cabin
Please remove shoes at the entrance
Quiet hours: 23:00 - 07:00
Hot tub cover must be closed with provided strap after use! heavy winds can appear on short notice and blow the lid away, can result in damage charge!
Separate waste into recycling bins provided
No pets allowed
Maximum 2 guests per cabin (+infant)
Privacy of other Guests, Please do not climb on the hills next to your house, these hills are made to separate the houses and give privacy to both you and other guests.  Respect the privacy of guests staying in other cabins and quiet hours !
Drone use, Drone use is forbidden at the property, but can be allowed under special requirements, always reach out to us for permission to use a drone ahead!
Maximum 4 guest per cabin (+infant)
No food or drinks beside still water inside the sauna chambers !
No food or added continements in the Jacuzzi such as (not limited to) oils, soaps, bath bombs etc
Its forbidden to climb on the grass hills next to Glass cabins, these hills are made for privacy of the guest, respect other guests privacy at all times!

title: Nordurljós Cabin.
content:
Welcome to Nordurljós Cabin! You're staying beneath the mighty Eyjafjöll mountains with views of Eyjafjallajökull glacier. Seljalandsfoss waterfall is just 15 minutes away. Don't forget to check the northern lights forecast!!

112

No smoking inside the cabin
Please remove shoes at the entrance
Quiet hours: 23:00 - 07:00
Hot tub cover must be closed after use
Separate waste into recycling bins provided
Maximum 6 guests per cabin
